analysis.summary: Home Assistant is an open-source home automation platform that provides local control and privacy for IoT devices. It processes device integrations, manages user authentication, serves a web UI, and orchestrates automation rules through a modular architecture supporting thousands of device types.
capabilities: Manages user authentication and authorization with multiple MFA modules (homeassistant/auth/)
Processes device integrations through component system with 2000+ integrations (homeassistant/components/)
Serves REST API endpoints for device control and state management (homeassistant/components/api/)
Provides WebSocket interface for real-time updates (homeassistant/components/websocket_api/)
Executes automation rules and triggers based on device states and events
Manages configuration through YAML files and UI-based config flows
Handles media streaming through Go2RTC integration (homeassistant/components/go2rtc/)
Processes voice commands and intents through conversation system
integrations: FFmpeg for media processing (ha-ffmpeg 3.2.2 in requirements.txt)
Various IoT device manufacturers (homeassistant/brands/ directory with 100+ brand files)

api_endpoints.path: REST /api/* — Device control and state management APIs

technical_facts: Manages user authentication and authorization with multiple MFA modules (homeassistant/auth/)
Processes device integrations through component system with 2000+ integrations (homeassistant/components/)
Serves REST API endpoints for device control and state management (homeassistant/components/api/)
Provides WebSocket interface for real-time updates (homeassistant/components/websocket_api/)
Executes automation rules and triggers based on device states and events
Manages configuration through YAML files and UI-based config flows
Handles media streaming through Go2RTC integration (homeassistant/components/go2rtc/)
Processes voice commands and intents through conversation system

architecture_patterns: Concurrency: async/await with asyncio event loop for handling concurrent device communications and API requests
deployment_options: CI/CD: GitHub Actions (inferred from CODEOWNERS and repository structure)

functional_dna.logic_archetype_evidence: Executes automation rules and triggers based on device states and events, manages configuration through YAML files and UI-based config flows, processes device integrations through component system with 2000+ integrations
functional_dna.data_contract_evidence: YAML configuration support via PyYAML 6.0.3 (requirements.txt), manages configuration through YAML files and UI-based config flows, SQLAlchemy 2.0.41 for database ORM (requirements.txt)
functional_dna.core_algorithmic_evidence: SQLAlchemy 2.0.41 for database ORM (requirements.txt), serves REST API endpoints for device control and state management (homeassistant/components/api/), supports backup and restore functionality (homeassistant/backup_restore.py)
functional_dna.concurrency_evidence: asyncio for concurrency (homeassistant/__main__.py, core.py), provides WebSocket interface for real-time updates (homeassistant/components/websocket_api/), aiohttp 3.13.3 for HTTP server (requirements.txt)
functional_dna.repurposing_reasoning: High repurposing potential due to modular component system with 2000+ integrations, well-defined API structure, and event-driven orchestration patterns. The stateful orchestrator pattern is applicable to many domains requiring coordination of distributed systems.

evidence_claims.claim: Uses SQLAlchemy ORM for database operations

evidence_claims.evidence: Serves REST API endpoints for device control and state management (homeassistant/components/api/), provides WebSocket interface for real-time updates (homeassistant/components/websocket_api/)
